authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.50(b)(22) — By-Day Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (22) By-Day Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.): "Closed to all fishing all year."

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet, updated June 1, 2026)
sources.quotedText: (22) By-Day Creek and tributaries (Mono Co.). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: CDFW — By-Day Creek Ecological Reserve (Places to Visit)
sources.quotedText: Hwy 395 about 4.5 mi west of Bridgeport, follow dirt road west of Bridgeport Ranger Station to the reserve.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: By-Day Creek and all its tributaries are closed to all fishing year-round; the creek and its Lahontan cutthroat trout population lie within the By-Day Creek Ecological Reserve.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year on By-Day Creek and its tributaries.
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Note: this water is closed to all fishing year-round, so no license permits take here.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+ (statewide requirement; moot here since the water is closed to fishing)
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
